recipes.title: Homemade Lasagna
recipes.description: Layers of comfort in every bite with my signature meat sauce and creamy ricotta.
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Sauté onion and garlic until softened, about 3-4 minutes.
Add ground beef and cook until browned, breaking it up as it cooks, about 8-10 minutes. Drain excess fat.
Stir in tomato paste and cook for 1 minute.
Add crushed tomatoes, oregano, basil, salt, and pepper. Simmer for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
While sauce simmers, cook lasagna noodles according to package directions. Drain and set aside.
In a bowl, mix ricotta, 1 cup mozzarella, parmesan, egg, salt, and pepper.
In a 9x13 inch baking dish, spread a thin layer of meat sauce on the bottom.
Layer noodles, ricotta mixture, and meat sauce. Repeat layers, ending with sauce on top.
Top with remaining mozzarella cheese.
Cover with foil and bake for 25 minutes. Remove foil and bake for another 15-20 minutes until cheese is bubbly.
Let rest for 10 minutes before serving.

recipes.title: Chicken Parmesan
recipes.description: Crispy, golden-fried chicken topped with marinara and melted mozzarella.
ingredients: 1 lb spaghetti or pasta of choice
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
Place chicken breasts between plastic wrap and pound to 1/2-inch thickness.
Set up three shallow bowls: one with flour mixed with salt and pepper, one with beaten eggs, and one with panko mixed with parmesan.
Dredge each chicken breast in flour, shaking off excess. Dip in egg, then coat with panko mixture.
Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Fry chicken for 3-4 minutes on each side until golden. Transfer to a baking dish.
Spoon marinara sauce over each chicken breast. Top with mozzarella cheese.
Bake for 15-20 minutes until cheese is melted and bubbly.
Meanwhile, cook pasta according to package directions.
Serve chicken over pasta and garnish with fresh basil.

recipes.title: Sunday Meatballs
recipes.description: Tender, juicy meatballs simmered in homemade tomato sauce—a family tradition.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine ground beef, breadcrumbs, parmesan, eggs, 4 minced garlic cloves, parsley, oregano, salt, and pepper.
Mix gently until just combined. Form into 1 1/2-inch meatballs.
Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large pot over medium-high heat. Brown meatballs in batches, about 3-4 minutes per batch. Transfer to a plate.
In the same pot, heat remaining olive oil. Sauté diced onion and 4 minced garlic cloves for 3-4 minutes.
Add tomato paste and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Pour in crushed tomatoes and add sugar, salt, and pepper.
Return meatballs to the pot and bring to a simmer.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 30-40 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Cook spaghetti according to package directions.
Serve meatballs and sauce over spaghetti.

recipes.title: Pumpkin Pasta Sauce
recipes.description: Creamy, seasonal sauce perfect for autumn gatherings and special dinners.
instructions: Melt butter in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add diced onion and cook for 5 minutes until softened. Add garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in pumpkin puree and cook for 2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Pour in cream and chicken broth. Stir well to combine.
Add sage, thyme, nutmeg, salt, and pepper. Simmer for 10 minutes over low heat.
Stir in parmesan cheese until melted and smooth.
Meanwhile, cook pasta according to package directions. Drain and reserve 1 cup pasta water.
If sauce is too thick, add pasta water a little at a time until desired consistency is reached.
Toss pasta with sauce and serve immediately.
Garnish with fresh sage leaves and toasted pine nuts if desired.

recipes.title: New York Cheesecake
recipes.description: Creamy, decadent cheesecake with a perfectly baked graham cracker crust.
instructions: Preheat oven to 325°F (160°C). Wrap the outside of a 9-inch springform pan with foil.
Mix graham cracker crumbs, melted butter, and 1/4 cup sugar. Press into the bottom of the prepared pan.
Bake crust for 10 minutes. Remove and let cool slightly.
In a large bowl, beat softened cream cheese until smooth, about 2 minutes.
Gradually add 1 cup sugar and flour, beating until combined. Add vanilla extract.
Add eggs one at a time, beating on low speed after each addition until just combined. Do not overmix.
In a small bowl, mix sour cream and heavy cream. Gently fold into cream cheese mixture.
Pour filling over crust.
Place the springform pan in a larger roasting pan. Add hot water to the roasting pan until it reaches halfway up the sides of the springform pan (water bath).
Bake for 55-70 minutes. The center should jiggle slightly when gently shaken.
Turn off the oven and crack the door open about 4 inches. Let cheesecake sit in the oven for 1 hour.
Remove from oven and refrigerate for at least 4 hours, preferably overnight.
Remove foil, run a thin knife around the edges, and release the springform.
Slice with a hot, wet knife and serve.

recipes.title: Chicken Pot Pie
recipes.description: Warm, comforting classic with tender chicken and vegetables in a creamy sauce.
ingredients: 2 sheets puff pastry or pie dough
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
Melt butter in a large pot over medium heat. Sauté onion, garlic, celery, carrots, and mushrooms for 8-10 minutes until softened.
Sprinkle flour over vegetables and stir for 1 minute to create a roux.
Gradually pour in chicken broth, stirring constantly to avoid lumps. Bring to a simmer.
Reduce heat and simmer for 5 minutes. Stir in cream and add cooked chicken, peas, thyme, salt, and pepper.
Simmer for 5 minutes until heated through. Taste and adjust seasonings.
Pour filling into a 9x13 inch baking dish or individual baking dishes.
Top with puff pastry or pie dough, sealing the edges.
Brush with egg wash and cut a few slits to allow steam to escape.
Bake for 25-30 minutes until crust is golden brown.
Let rest for 5 minutes before serving.
